[Home](./index.md) &gt; [@fgv/ts-chocolate](./ts-chocolate.md) &gt; [LibraryRuntime](./ts-chocolate.libraryruntime.md) &gt; [Procedure](./ts-chocolate.libraryruntime.procedure.md)

## LibraryRuntime.Procedure class

A resolved view of a procedure with proper task resolution.

Procedure wraps a data-layer Procedure and provides: - Composite identity (ProcedureId) for cross-source references - Proper task resolution (not placeholders like the data-layer) - Computed timing properties

Unlike the data-layer Procedure.render() which returns `[Task: taskId]` placeholders, Procedure.render() actually resolves task references and renders their templates.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export declare class Procedure implements IProcedure 
```
**Implements:** [IProcedure](./ts-chocolate.libraryruntime.iprocedure.md)

</td><td>

</td><td>

Factory method for creating a Procedure.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[render(renderContext)](./ts-chocolate.libraryruntime.procedure.render.md)


</td><td>


</td><td>

Renders the procedure with the given context. Resolves task references to actual task content (not placeholders).

This is the main difference from the data-layer Procedure.render(): - Data layer: Returns `[Task: taskId]` placeholders for task references - Runtime layer: Actually resolves task references and renders templates


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
